extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won stage 2 sprint after POGAČAR took intermediate sprint bonus seconds to claim virtual GC lead.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR contested flat intermediate sprint with UAE domestique leadout showing GC aggression for bonus seconds 12.5km from finish.
Bora-Hansgrohe initiated echelon split at 70.5km but peloton reunited, suggesting insufficient crosswind commitment from other teams.
Multiple sprinter teams lost positioning in 90kph downhill finale suggesting descending speed matters more than pure leadout trains.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Sprinting for intermediate bonuses on flat stages reveals willingness to micro-manage GC gaps rather than rely solely on climbing
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Won through middle gap after leadout disintegrated, showing opportunistic finishing beats positional discipline in chaotic finales
rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: neutral: Second place but caught out by PEDERSEN's improvisation suggests pure speed insufficient when sprint organization collapses
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Third at intermediate sprint and top-10 finish demonstrates dual-purpose value as bonus hunter and safety finisher
rider_signals.VAN HOOYDONCK Nathan: positive: Fourth at intermediate sprint 12.5km out shows UAE's leadout depth extends to placement riders for POGAČAR
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Relayed crosswind message to UAE showing domestique communication role rather than attacking instincts on windy flat stage
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: Lost VAN POPPEL wheel inside 500m indicating positioning fragility when leadout fractures in technical finales
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: Already caught with 500m remaining suggests poor pre-finale positioning or insufficient acceleration in chaotic sprint buildup
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: Crash created nervousness but team support brought him back, showing GC vulnerability to non-wind incidents on nervous stages
